vulnerability.title: Install AIDE
vulnerability.description: The aide package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install aide
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "aide" ; then dnf install -y "aide" fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Installing the AIDE package is a standard, low-risk operation that does not affect authentication, networking, or filesystem configuration. It follows the STIG hardening pattern for package installation and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential dependency conflicts if the system is heavily customized

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The proposed command `dnf install -y aide` will install the AIDE package and satisfy the OpenSCAP rule. It is a straightforward and safe approach for the target platform.
concerns: The plan assumes root privileges are available; if run as a non‑elevated user, it will fail.
suggested_improvements: Add an explicit check to see if aide is already installed before running the install; e.g., `rpm -q aide || dnf install -y aide`.
Verify installation after the command with `rpm -q aide` or `dnf list installed aide` to confirm success.
Consider adding `--nobest` or `--repo` options if a specific repository is preferred.
Ensure the command is run under sudo or as root to avoid permission errors.
